executive_summary: Canada is experiencing an unprecedented ransomware surge with 103 Canadian organizations compromised in a single day on June 26, 2026. High-profile victims include Canada Goose, National Money Mart (database leaked), Peterborough Public Health, Eastern Townships School Board, Municipality of North Perth, and the MRC de Maskinongé district government. The Qilin ransomware group alone claimed 21 victims, followed by Play (11), Safepay (11), and Clop (13). Critical sectors hit include public health, education, financial services, manufacturing, and municipal government—indicating a coordinated nationwide campaign.

items.name: Qilin
items.targets: 21 Canadian organizations including Eastern Townships School Board, Workers Health & Safety Centre, NovAtel (Hexagon subsidiary), BioPharma Services, Waterloo Information Systems, Traffic Tech, Service Star Freightways, and multiple small-to-medium businesses across technology, construction, manufacturing, and professional services sectors
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1657 (Financial Theft), T1589 (Gather Victim Identity Information)

items.name: Play
items.targets: 11 Canadian organizations including Equine Canada, Chemical Computing Group, Makivik, Security ONE Alarm Systems, Pewarchuk CPA, and manufacturing firms
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1490 (Inhibit System Recovery)

items.name: Safepay
items.targets: 11 Canadian organizations including Teccart educational institution, agriculture/food production firms (Matt and Steve, ripobec.com, John O's Foods), construction companies, and manufacturers
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1489 (Service Stop)

items.name: Clop
items.targets: 13 Canadian organizations including SMT Lawyers, Cloud Data Works, Sensible Solutions, CMH Helicopters, Ideal Welders, and hospitality/energy sectors
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service), T1190 (Exploit Public-Facing Application)

items.name: TheGentlemen
items.targets: 5 Canadian organizations including Labtician Ophthalmics (healthcare), Groupe Caddac (construction), ControlGMC (manufacturing), Real Tech, and Colliers
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1041 (Exfiltration Over C2 Channel)

items.name: DragonForce
items.targets: 6 Canadian organizations including Emond Publishing (education), McKee-Pownall Equine Services (healthcare), Phoenix Labs, CCR Solutions, Esposito Bros Construction, and Soteck
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1021 (Remote Services)

items.name: Lynx
items.targets: 5 Canadian organizations including Peterborough Public Health (public sector), Trucash (financial services), KTL Group, Laurysen Kitchens, and Group Echo
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1078 (Valid Accounts)

items.name: IncRansom
items.targets: 7 Canadian organizations including Bais Yaakov school (education), CPK Interior, healthcare (Bison Family Medical), accounting firms, and manufacturing companies
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1562 (Impair Defenses)

items.name: AiLock
items.targets: 2 Canadian organizations: Eeyou Communications Network (telecommunications) and Solutions Extreme Technology
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact)

items.name: Sinobi
items.targets: 3 Canadian organizations: Kids & Company (childcare), Active Green + Ross (automotive services), Morison Insurance Brokers
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1566 (Phishing)

items.name: Everest
items.targets: National Money Mart Company - Database leaked (financial services)
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1005 (Data from Local System), T1041 (Exfiltration Over C2 Channel)

items.name: Medusa
items.targets: MRC de Maskinongé district government (public sector)
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1078 (Valid Accounts)

items.name: Worldleaks
items.targets: Municipality of North Perth (public sector)
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1078 (Valid Accounts)

items.name: Devman
items.targets: 4 Canadian organizations including Axion50plus (financial services), Ravand (technology), and agricultural cooperatives
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact)

items.name: Nightspire
items.targets: 2 Canadian organizations: Regal Building Materials (construction) and an unidentified LLP
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact)

items.name: Akira
items.targets: Inland Audio Visual (business services)
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1490 (Inhibit System Recovery)

items.name: Coinbase Cartel
items.targets: Canada Goose (consumer services)
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1657 (Financial Theft)

items.name: Securotrop
items.targets: 2 Canadian organizations: Living Realty (real estate), Cadman Power Equipment (manufacturing)
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact)

items.name: Bravox
items.targets: John O's Foods (agriculture/food production)
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact)

canada_landscape.summary: Canada suffered a catastrophic ransomware event on June 26, 2026 with 103 organizations breached across all provinces and sectors. Critical infrastructure targets include Peterborough Public Health, Eastern Townships School Board, Municipality of North Perth, MRC de Maskinongé district, Eeyou Communications Network (telecom), and Workers Health & Safety Centre. No CCCS advisories were identified in the data feed.
canada_landscape.retailers: Canada Goose (compromised by Coinbase Cartel), Active Green + Ross automotive services (Sinobi), Kids & Company childcare chain (Sinobi)
canada_landscape.financial: National Money Mart Company - database leaked by Everest ransomware group; Morison Insurance Brokers (Sinobi); Trucash financial services (Lynx); Axion50plus credit union (Devman); multiple accounting/CPA firms including Pewarchuk CPA, MG CPA, and Reidhurst Nagy

report_2w.executive_summary: FortiBleed credential leak threatens thousands of Fortinet devices with compromised credentials, prompting an official CCCS advisory (AL26-014). LastPass suffered a supply chain breach via Klue, exposing customer support data through stolen OAuth tokens—the company's latest in a series of security incidents. Scattered Spider members pleaded guilty to the £39M Transport for London cyberattack. Malicious npm packages impersonating PostCSS tools are delivering Windows RATs to developers. Five Eyes agencies warn AI is accelerating cyber threats and zero-day exploitation.

items.cia_impact: Confidentiality: High, Integrity: Medium, Availability: High

items.description: Apache HTTP Server path traversal and remote code execution
items.cia_impact: Confidentiality: High, Integrity: High, Availability: High
Confidentiality: High, Integrity: High, Availability: High
Confidentiality: High, Integrity: High, Availability: Medium

items.description: Zephyr Bluetooth Host ISO path missing SDU header length checks causing denial of service
items.cia_impact: Confidentiality: Low, Integrity: Low, Availability: High

items.name: Scattered Spider
items.targets: Transport for London (£39M cyberattack)
items.ttps: T1566 Phishing, T1078 Valid Accounts, T1485 Data Destruction, T1486 Data Encrypted for Impact

items.name: DragonForce
items.targets: Various organizations using Microsoft Teams as C2
items.ttps: T1102 Web Service (Microsoft Teams as C2 channel), T1486 Data Encrypted for Impact (ransomware)

items.name: Icarus
items.targets: Klue (supply chain attack affecting LastPass, Huntress, Recorded Future, Tanium and others)
items.ttps: T1199 Trusted Relationship, T1550.001 Application Access Token (stolen OAuth tokens), T1213 Data from Information Repositories (Salesforce CRM data exfiltration)

items.name: Sapphire Sleet
items.targets: JavaScript/npm ecosystem developers
items.ttps: T1195.002 Supply Chain Compromise (malicious npm packages), T1059.007 Command and Scripting Interpreter: JavaScript, T1027 Obfuscated Files or Information

items.name: Akira (ransomware group)
items.targets: IH Engineers, Leo International, multiple organizations
items.ttps: T1486 Data Encrypted for Impact, T1490 Inhibit System Recovery, T1657 Financial Theft

items.name: SocGholish
items.targets: Various organizations via drive-by download attacks (active 2017-2026)
items.ttps: T1189 Drive-by Compromise, T1036 Masquerading (fake browser updates), T1204.002 User Execution: Malicious File

items.name: DBHunter
items.targets: Kosmetikschule Schaefer (German cosmetics school), various organizations
items.ttps: T1530 Data from Cloud Storage, T1087 Account Discovery, T1213 Data from Information Repositories

items.name: FulcrumSec (extortion group)
items.targets: Novo Nordisk (claimed 1TB+ data theft including drug information)
items.ttps: T1567 Exfiltration Over Web Service, T1657 Financial Theft, T1486 Data Encrypted for Impact

items.name: ShinyHunters
items.targets: Kodak (limited company data exposure)
items.ttps: T1078 Valid Accounts, T1530 Data from Cloud Storage, T1567 Exfiltration Over Web Service

canada_landscape.summary: CCCS issued Alert AL26-014 warning of FortiBleed—a leak of thousands of compromised Fortinet device credentials requiring immediate credential rotation. Five Eyes agencies (including Canada) jointly warned that AI is accelerating cyber threats and enabling faster zero-day exploitation, urging CSOs to adapt risk strategies. No direct breaches of major Canadian retailers or financial institutions were reported in this period.
